trajectory.content: I'm just checking — what happens if I need to change my flight? What are my options and fees?

I can help explain your options, but I can only give exact change rules and fees for a specific booking if you want me to look it up.

Conservative interpretation of the policy: “change options and fees” can be general information without taking action; to retrieve booking-specific rules, I’d need your booking reference and your permission to check it.

In general, if you need to change a flight, your options usually depend on:
- Fare type: some fares are flexible, others have change fees or restrictions.
- Route and airline rules: domestic/international rules can differ.
- Timing: changes closer to departure may have fewer options.
- Seat/class availability: you can usually only move to flights with available seats in the applicable fare/class.
- Fare difference: even if no change fee applies, you may still need to pay any increase in fare.

Typical outcomes:
- Free change: on flexible tickets or special waivers.
- Change fee plus fare difference: common on more restrictive fares.
- Fare difference only: some tickets no longer charge a separate change fee.
- Not changeable: some basic or promotional fares may not allow changes.
- Same-day change/standby: sometimes available for a lower fixed fee or free for eligible passengers.

Possible options if you need to change:
- Change date or time on the same route
- Change to an earlier/later flight the same day
- Change destination/origin, if fare rules allow
- Upgrade cabin while changing, if seats are available
- Cancel and rebook instead, if that works out better under the fare rules

What I can do next:
- If you want general guidance only, I can tell you what to check before changing.
- If you want exact options and fees for your ticket, send your booking reference and I can look up your booking details.
